qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: What is the worst-case number of arithmetic operations performed by recursive binary search on a sorted array of size ? A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: For which of the following inputs does binary search take time in the worst case? A. An array of integers in any order B. A linked list of integers in any order C. An array of integers in increasing order D. A linked list of integers in increasing order

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Let denote the maximum number of comparisons made while searching for an entry in a sorted array of size using binary search. Which ONE of the following options is TRUE? B. D. A. C. Bitonic Array (1)

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: An array of length with distinct elements is said to be bitonic if there is an index such that is sorted in the non-decreasing order and is sorted in the non-increasing order. Which ONE of the following represents the best possible asymptotic bound for the worst-case number of comparisons by an algo
